caption: A silver fuel tanker is stopped on the shoulder of a rural highway with a dark puddle of leaked liquid pooling on the asphalt below its rear hose; a brush fire burns in the dry grass off the roadside roughly fifty feet ahead, and the driver stands beside the cab on a phone looking toward the fire.
annotator_notes: Image matches prompt. MUTUAL-HAZARD RULE applied (cross-class mechanism): spreading brush fire and leaking fuel mutually worsen each other — fire can ignite the leak (explosion / fireball), and the leak provides fresh combustible mass (vapor + liquid flow toward flame) that intensifies the fire. Both directions emitted. Driver_1 stands between tanker and fire on the road; tanker_1's leaked fuel on the asphalt is within feet of him (may_harm via slip / chemical contact / proximity to imminent explosion). Direct fire_1->driver_1 deferred per distance rule — fire is still on grass roadside ~50 ft away; risk to driver flows primarily through the tanker-explosion path.
